mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 8.0% signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output growth, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >58% of observations when INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or the regime-conditional alpha is indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a stable foundation for consumer spending, generating consistently positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of periods, or the point-biserial correlation between payroll growth and 5-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising unemployment indicate accommodative policy during economic weakness, which boosts equity risk premiums and produces positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of cases when the joint condition is met, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% signals economic stagnation, which erodes corporate profit expectations and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in >57% of periods following sub-1% GDP growth, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a disinflationary phase confirms supply-side recovery, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 over any 3-year out-of-sample period.
